title: ffi/identity.rs: save_identity_to_file does not validate the 64-byte content as a valid key before writing
description: save_identity_to_file decodes base64 and checks len==64, then writes the raw bytes. It does not call Identity::from_secret_bytes to validate that the content represents a valid (non-equal-seeds) identity. A caller could save a 64-byte blob where bytes[0..32] == bytes[32..64] (equal seeds), which would be written and later rejected by load + from_secret_bytes. The file would be corrupt on first save. Calling Identity::from_secret_bytes as a validation step before writing would catch this and is cheap.
